A2-MAE_A_spatial-temporal-spectral_unifi.title: A2-MAE: A Spatial–Temporal–Spectral Unified Remote Sensing Pretraining Method Based on Anchor-Aware Masked Autoencoder
A2-MAE_A_spatial-temporal-spectral_unifi.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
A2-MAE_A_spatial-temporal-spectral_unifi.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
A2-MAE_A_spatial-temporal-spectral_unifi.tldr: An anchor-aware masked autoencoder (A2-MAE) method, leveraging intrinsic complementary information from the different kinds of images (featuring different resolutions, spectral compositions, and acquisition times) and geo-information to reconstruct the masked patches during the pretraining phase to unlock the potential of RS data.

A_Sentinel-3_foundation_model_for_ocean_.title: A Sentinel-3 foundation model for ocean colour
A_Sentinel-3_foundation_model_for_ocean_.tldr: The utility of self-trained FMs for marine monitoring, in particular for making use of small amounts of high quality labelled data and in capturing detailed spatial patterns of ocean colour whilst matching point observations, are demonstrated.

AnySat_An_Earth_Observation_Model_for_An.title: AnySat: One Earth Observation Model for Many Resolutions, Scales, and Modalities
AnySat_An_Earth_Observation_Model_for_An.journal: 2025 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
AnySat_An_Earth_Observation_Model_for_An.tldr: AnySat, a multimodal model based on joint embedding predictive architecture (JEPA) and scale-adaptive spatial encoders, allowing a single model to train a single model on highly heterogeneous data in a self-supervised manner is proposed.

Bridging_Remote_Sensors_with_Multisensor.title: Bridging Remote Sensors with Multisensor Geospatial Foundation Models
Bridging_Remote_Sensors_with_Multisensor.journal: 2024 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)

CACo_Change-Aware_Sampling_and_Contrasti.title: Change-Aware Sampling and Contrastive Learning for Satellite Images
CACo_Change-Aware_Sampling_and_Contrasti.journal: 2023 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)

CROMA_Remote_Sensing_Representations_wit.title: CROMA: Remote Sensing Representations with Contrastive Radar-Optical Masked Autoencoders
CROMA_Remote_Sensing_Representations_wit.tldr: This work presents CROMA: a framework that combines contrastive and reconstruction self-supervised objectives to learn rich unimodal and multimodal representations, and introduces X- and 2D-ALiBi, which spatially biases the authors' cross- and self-attention matrices.

CSP_Self-Supervised_Contrastive_Spatial_.title: CSP: Self-Supervised Contrastive Spatial Pre-Training for Geospatial-Visual Representations
CSP_Self-Supervised_Contrastive_Spatial_.tldr: This work presents Contrastive Spatial Pre-Training (CSP), a self-supervised learning framework for geo-tagged images that uses a dual-encoder to separately encode the images and their corresponding geo-locations, and uses contrastive objectives to learn effective location representations from images, which can be transferred to downstream supervised tasks such as image classification.

Changen2_Multi-Temporal_Remote_Sensing_G.title: Changen2: Multi-Temporal Remote Sensing Generative Change Foundation Model
Changen2_Multi-Temporal_Remote_Sensing_G.venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
Changen2_Multi-Temporal_Remote_Sensing_G.journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
Changen2_Multi-Temporal_Remote_Sensing_G.tldr: This paper presents Changen2, a GPCM implemented with a resolution-scalable diffusion transformer which can generate time series of remote sensing images and corresponding semantic and change labels from labeled and even unlabeled single-temporal images, a “generative change foundation model” that can be trained at scale via self-supervision, and is capable of producing change supervisory signals from unlabeled single-temporal images.

Cross-Scale_MAE_A_Tale_of_Multiscale_Exp.title: Cross-Scale MAE: A Tale of Multi-Scale Exploitation in Remote Sensing
Cross-Scale_MAE_A_Tale_of_Multiscale_Exp.tldr: This paper presents Cross-Scale MAE, a self-supervised model built upon the Masked Auto-Encoder (MAE), which employs scale augmentation techniques and enforces cross-scale consistency constraints through both contrastive and generative losses to ensure consistent and meaningful representations well-suited for a wide range of downstream tasks.

Cross-Sensor_Self-Supervised_Training_an.title: Cross-Sensor Self-Supervised Training and Alignment for Remote Sensing
Cross-Sensor_Self-Supervised_Training_an.venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
Cross-Sensor_Self-Supervised_Training_an.journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
Cross-Sensor_Self-Supervised_Training_an.tldr: This work designs a self-supervised training loss, the multi-sensor alignment dense loss, to align representations across sensors, even with vastly different resolutions, through a contrastive patch-wise mechanism, and demonstrates that X-STARS outperforms the state-of-the-art with less data across various conditions of data availability and resolutions.

CrossEarth_Geospatial_Vision_Foundation_.title: CrossEarth: Geospatial Vision Foundation Model for Domain Generalizable Remote Sensing Semantic Segmentation
CrossEarth_Geospatial_Vision_Foundation_.venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
CrossEarth_Geospatial_Vision_Foundation_.journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
CrossEarth_Geospatial_Vision_Foundation_.tldr: The first vision foundation model for RSDG semantic segmentation, CrossEarth, is introduced, demonstrating strong cross-domain generalization through a specially designed data-level Earth-Style Injection pipeline and a model-level Multi-Task Training pipeline.

CtxMIM_Context-Enhanced_Masked_Image_Mod.title: CtxMIM: Context-Enhanced Masked Image Modeling for Remote Sensing Image Understanding
CtxMIM_Context-Enhanced_Masked_Image_Mod.venue: ACM Trans. Multim. Comput. Commun. Appl.
CtxMIM_Context-Enhanced_Masked_Image_Mod.journal: ACM Transactions on Multimedia Computing, Communications and Applications
CtxMIM_Context-Enhanced_Masked_Image_Mod.tldr: Results demonstrate that CtxMIM learns impressive remote sensing representations with high generalization and transferability and outperform fully supervised and state-of-the-art self-supervised learning methods on various downstream tasks, including land cover classification, semantic segmentation, object detection, and instance segmentation.

DINO-MC_Self-supervised_Contrastive_Lear.title: Extending global-local view alignment for self-supervised learning with remote sensing imagery
DINO-MC_Self-supervised_Contrastive_Lear.venue: 2024 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition Workshops (CVPRW)
DINO-MC_Self-supervised_Contrastive_Lear.journal: 2024 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition Workshops (CVPRW)
DINO-MC_Self-supervised_Contrastive_Lear.tldr: Inspired by DINO, DINO-MC is extended and proposed which uses local views of various sized crops instead of a single fixed size in order to alleviate the limited variation in object size observed in remote sensing imagery.

DOFA_Neural_Plasticity-Inspired_Foundati.title: Neural Plasticity-Inspired Multimodal Foundation Model for Earth Observation
DOFA_Neural_Plasticity-Inspired_Foundati.tldr: The Dynamic One-For-All (DOFA) model is proposed, a unified, multimodal foundation framework designed for diverse vision tasks in EO, Inspired by neural plasticity, which achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple downstream tasks and generalizes well to unseen modalities.

DeCUR_Decoupling_Common_and_Unique_Repre.title: Decoupling Common and Unique Representations for Multimodal Self-supervised Learning
DeCUR_Decoupling_Common_and_Unique_Repre.tldr: Decoupling Common and Unique Representations (DeCUR) is proposed, a simple yet effective method for multimodal self-supervised learning that distinguishing inter- and intra-modal embeddings through multimodal redundancy reduction, can integrate complementary information across different modalities.

DeepAndes_A_Self-Supervised_Vision_Found.title: DeepAndes: A Self-Supervised Vision Foundation Model for Multispectral Remote Sensing Imagery of the Andes
DeepAndes_A_Self-Supervised_Vision_Found.venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
DeepAndes_A_Self-Supervised_Vision_Found.journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
DeepAndes_A_Self-Supervised_Vision_Found.tldr: DeepAndes is introduced, a transformer-based vision foundation model trained on three million multispectral satellite images, specifically tailored for Andean archaeology, marking the first foundation model designed explicitly for the Andes region.

DiffusionSat_A_Generative_Foundation_Mod.title: DiffusionSat: A Generative Foundation Model for Satellite Imagery
DiffusionSat_A_Generative_Foundation_Mod.tldr: This paper presents DiffusionSat, to date the largest generative foundation model trained on a collection of publicly available large, high-resolution remote sensing datasets, and is the first large-scale generative foundation model for satellite imagery.

EarthDial_Turning_Multi-sensory_Earth_Ob.title: EarthDial: Turning Multi-sensory Earth Observations to Interactive Dialogues
EarthDial_Turning_Multi-sensory_Earth_Ob.journal: 2025 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
EarthDial_Turning_Multi-sensory_Earth_Ob.tldr: EarthDial is introduced, a conversational assistant specifically designed for Earth Observation (EO) data, transforming complex, multi-sensory Earth observations into interactive, natural language dialogues, and outperforms existing generic and domain-specific models, achieving better generalization across various EO tasks.

EarthGPT_A_Universal_Multi-modal_Large_L.title: EarthGPT: A Universal Multimodal Large Language Model for Multisensor Image Comprehension in Remote Sensing Domain
EarthGPT_A_Universal_Multi-modal_Large_L.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
EarthGPT_A_Universal_Multi-modal_Large_L.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
EarthGPT_A_Universal_Multi-modal_Large_L.tldr: The EarthGPT’s superior performance in various RS visual interpretation tasks compared with the other specialist models and MLLMs is demonstrated, proving the effectiveness of the proposed EarthGPT and offering a versatile paradigm for open-set reasoning tasks.

EarthPT_a_time_series_foundation_model_f.title: EarthPT: a time series foundation model for Earth Observation
EarthPT_a_time_series_foundation_model_f.tldr: It is demonstrated that EarthPT is an effective forecaster that can accurately predict future pixel-level surface reflectances across the 400-2300 nm range well into the future and that embeddings learnt by EarthPT hold semantically meaningful information and could be exploited for downstream tasks such as highly granular, dynamic land use classification.

EarthView_A_Large_Scale_Remote_Sensing_D.title: EarthView: A Large Scale Remote Sensing Dataset for Self-Supervision
EarthView_A_Large_Scale_Remote_Sensing_D.venue: 2025 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision Workshops (WACVW)
EarthView_A_Large_Scale_Remote_Sensing_D.journal: 2025 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision Workshops (WACVW)
EarthView_A_Large_Scale_Remote_Sensing_D.tldr: This paper presents EarthView, a comprehensive dataset specifically designed for self-supervision on remote sensing data, intended to enhance deep learning applications on Earth monitoring tasks, and introduces Earth-MAE, a tailored Masked Autoencoder developed to tackle the distinct challenges of remote sensing data.

Embedding_Earth_Self-supervised_contrast.title: Embedding Earth: Self-supervised contrastive pre-training for dense land cover classification
Embedding_Earth_Self-supervised_contrast.tldr: Embedding Earth a self-supervised contrastive pre-training method for leveraging the large availability of satellite imagery to improve performance on downstream dense land cover classification tasks and finds that learnt features can generalize between disparate regions opening up the possibility of using the proposed pre- training scheme as a replacement to random initialization for Earth observation tasks.

FLAVARS_A_Multimodal_Foundational_Langua.title: FLAVARS: A Multimodal Foundational Language and Vision Alignment Model for Remote Sensing
FLAVARS_A_Multimodal_Foundational_Langua.tldr: FLAVARS is proposed, a pretraining method that combines the best of both contrastive learning and masked modeling, along with geospatial alignment via contrastive location encoding, and significantly outperforms a baseline of SkyCLIP for vision-only tasks such as KNN classification and semantic segmentation.

Falcon_A_Remote_Sensing_Vision-Language_.title: Falcon: A Remote Sensing Vision-Language Foundation Model
Falcon_A_Remote_Sensing_Vision-Language_.tldr: A holistic vision-language foundation model tailored for remote sensing, named Falcon, which offers a unified, prompt-based paradigm that effectively executes comprehensive and complex remote sensing tasks.

Feature_Guided_Masked_Autoencoder_for_Se.title: Feature Guided Masked Autoencoder for Self-Supervised Learning in Remote Sensing
Feature_Guided_Masked_Autoencoder_for_Se.venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
Feature_Guided_Masked_Autoencoder_for_Se.journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
Feature_Guided_Masked_Autoencoder_for_Se.tldr: Spectral and spatial remote sensing image features are explored as improved MAE-reconstruction targets and feature guided MAE (FG-MAE) is proposed: reconstructing a combination of histograms of oriented gradients and normalized difference indices for multispectral images, and reconstructing HOG for SAR images.

FlexiMo_A_Flexible_Remote_Sensing_Founda.title: FlexiMo: A Flexible Remote Sensing Foundation Model
FlexiMo_A_Flexible_Remote_Sensing_Founda.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
FlexiMo_A_Flexible_Remote_Sensing_Founda.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
FlexiMo_A_Flexible_Remote_Sensing_Founda.tldr: FlexiMo is proposed, a flexible RSFM that endows the pretrained model with the flexibility to adapt to arbitrary spatial resolutions and improves the practical deployability of RSFMs under real-world sensor heterogeneity and scale variations.

FoMo-Bench_a_multi-modal_multi-scale_and.title: FoMo: Multi-Modal, Multi-Scale and Multi-Task Remote Sensing Foundation Models for Forest Monitoring
FoMo-Bench_a_multi-modal_multi-scale_and.tldr: FoMo-Net is proposed, a pre-training framework to develop foundation models with the capacity to process any combination of commonly used modalities and spectral bands in remote sensing, and aims to inspire research collaborations between machine learning and forest biology researchers in exploring scalable multi-modal and multi-task models for forest monitoring and beyond.

Fus-MAE_A_cross-attention-based_data_fus.title: FUS-MAE: A Cross-Attention-Based Data Fusion Approach for Masked Autoencoders in Remote Sensing
Fus-MAE_A_cross-attention-based_data_fus.venue: IEEE International Geoscience and Remote Sensing Symposium
Fus-MAE_A_cross-attention-based_data_fus.journal: IGARSS 2024 - 2024 IEEE International Geoscience and Remote Sensing Symposium
Fus-MAE_A_cross-attention-based_data_fus.tldr: Fus-MAE is introduced, a self-supervised learning framework based on masked autoencoders that uses cross-attention to perform early and feature-level data fusion between synthetic aperture radar and multispectral optical data - two modalities with a significant domain gap.

GAIR_Improving_Multimodal_Geo-Foundation.title: GAIR: Location-aware self-supervised contrastive pre-training with geo-aligned implicit representations
GAIR_Improving_Multimodal_Geo-Foundation.venue: Isprs Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
GAIR_Improving_Multimodal_Geo-Foundation.journal: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
GAIR_Improving_Multimodal_Geo-Foundation.tldr: GAIR, a novel location-aware self-supervised learning (SSL) objective integrating overhead RS data, street view (SV) imagery, and their geolocation metadata, is introduced, which outperforms state-of-the-art geo-foundation models (GeoFM) and alternative SSL training objectives (e.g., MoCo V3 and MAE) that do not use fine-grained geo-aligned spatial representations.

Galileo_Learning_Global_Local_Features_o.title: Galileo: Learning Global & Local Features of Many Remote Sensing Modalities
Galileo_Learning_Global_Local_Features_o.tldr: This work presents a novel self-supervised learning algorithm that extracts multi-scale features across a flexible set of input modalities through masked modeling and introduces a highly multimodal transformer to represent many remote sensing modalities across space and time.

Generic_Knowledge_Boosted_Pre-training_F.title: Generic Knowledge Boosted Pretraining for Remote Sensing Images
Generic_Knowledge_Boosted_Pre-training_F.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
Generic_Knowledge_Boosted_Pre-training_F.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
Generic_Knowledge_Boosted_Pre-training_F.tldr: A novel RSP framework, generic knowledge boosted RSP (GeRSP), is proposed, to learn robust representations from RS and natural images for RS understanding tasks, and is evaluated on three downstream tasks, i.e., object detection, semantic segmentation, and scene classification.

GeoChat_Grounded_Large_Vision-Language_M.title: GeoChat:Grounded Large Vision-Language Model for Remote Sensing
GeoChat_Grounded_Large_Vision-Language_M.journal: 2024 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
GeoChat_Grounded_Large_Vision-Language_M.tldr: This work proposes GeoChat - the first versatile remote sensing VLM that offers multitask conversational capabilities with high-resolution RS images, and generates a novel RS multimodal instruction-following dataset by extending image-text pairs from existing diverse RS datasets.

GeoLangBind_Unifying_Earth_Observation_w.title: GeoLangBind: Unifying Earth Observation with Agglomerative Vision-Language Foundation Models
GeoLangBind_Unifying_Earth_Observation_w.tldr: This work presents GeoLangBind, a novel agglomerative vision--language foundation model that bridges the gap between heterogeneous EO data modalities using language as a unifying medium, and creates a powerful agglomerative foundation model that excels in both zero-shot vision--language comprehension and fine-grained visual understanding.

GeoViT_A_Versatile_Vision_Transformer_Ar.title: GeoViT: A Versatile Vision Transformer Architecture for Geospatial Image Analysis
GeoViT_A_Versatile_Vision_Transformer_Ar.tldr: GeoViT demonstrates the efficacy of vision transformer architectures in harnessing satellite-derived data for enhanced GHG emission insights, proving instrumental in advancing climate change monitoring and emission regulation efforts globally.

Global_and_Local_Contrastive_Self-Superv.title: Global and Local Contrastive Self-Supervised Learning for Semantic Segmentation of HR Remote Sensing Images
Global_and_Local_Contrastive_Self-Superv.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
Global_and_Local_Contrastive_Self-Superv.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
Global_and_Local_Contrastive_Self-Superv.tldr: A global style and local matching contrastive learning network (GLCNet) for RSI semantic segmentation and shows that this method mostly outperforms the state-of-the-art self-supervised methods and the ImageNet pretraining method when there are some differences between the datasets of upstream tasks and downstream tasks.

HyperFree_A_Channel-adaptive_and_Tuning-.title: HyperFree: A Channel-adaptive and Tuning-free Foundation Model for Hyperspectral Remote Sensing Imagery
HyperFree_A_Channel-adaptive_and_Tuning-.journal: 2025 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
HyperFree_A_Channel-adaptive_and_Tuning-.tldr: A tuning-free hyper-spectral foundation model called HyperFree, by adapting the existing visual prompt engineering, which can generate multiple semantic-aware masks for one prompt by treating feature distance as semantic-similarity and making the prompt design more tractable.

IaI-SimCLR_Multi-Modal_Multi-Objective_C.title: Multi-Modal Multi-Objective Contrastive Learning for Sentinel-1/2 Imagery
IaI-SimCLR_Multi-Modal_Multi-Objective_C.venue: 2023 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition Workshops (CVPRW)
IaI-SimCLR_Multi-Modal_Multi-Objective_C.journal: 2023 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition Workshops (CVPRW)

In-domain_representation_learning_for_re.title: In-domain representation learning for remote sensing
In-domain_representation_learning_for_re.tldr: This work investigates in-domain representation learning to develop generic remote sensing representations and explores which characteristics are important for a dataset to be a good source for remote sensing representation learning.

LHRS-Bot_Empowering_Remote_Sensing_with_.title: LHRS-Bot: Empowering Remote Sensing with VGI-Enhanced Large Multimodal Language Model
LHRS-Bot_Empowering_Remote_Sensing_with_.tldr: LHRS-Bot is introduced, an MLLM tailored for RS image understanding through a novel multi-level vision-language alignment strategy and a curriculum learning method, and LHRS-Bench is introduced, a benchmark for thoroughly evaluating MLLMs' abilities in RS image understanding.

MA3E_Masked_Angle-Aware_Autoencoder_for_.title: Masked Angle-Aware Autoencoder for Remote Sensing Images
MA3E_Masked_Angle-Aware_Autoencoder_for_.tldr: The Masked Angle-Aware Autoencoder (MA3E) is proposed to perceive and learn angles during pre-training to effectively learn rotation-invariant representations by restoring the angle variation introduced on the rotated crop.

MM-VSF_Towards_Knowledge_Guided_Pretrain.title: Towards Knowledge Guided Pretraining Approaches for Multimodal Foundation Models: Applications in Remote Sensing
MM-VSF_Towards_Knowledge_Guided_Pretrain.venue: ACM Transactions on Spatial Algorithms and Systems
MM-VSF_Towards_Knowledge_Guided_Pretrain.journal: ACM Transactions on Spatial Algorithms and Systems
MM-VSF_Towards_Knowledge_Guided_Pretrain.tldr: Knowledge Guided Variable-Step Forecasting (KG-VSF), a novel pretraining task that models forecasting as a conditional generation task, leads to strong embeddings which give enhanced performance when finetuned on downstream tasks where capturing this causality matters is important.

MTP_Advancing_Remote_Sensing_Foundation_.title: MTP: Advancing Remote Sensing Foundation Model via Multitask Pretraining
MTP_Advancing_Remote_Sensing_Foundation_.venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
MTP_Advancing_Remote_Sensing_Foundation_.journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
MTP_Advancing_Remote_Sensing_Foundation_.tldr: This study explores the multitask pretraining (MTP) paradigm for RS foundation models to address task discrepancy and demonstrates the superiority of the models over existing ones of similar size and their competitive performance compared to larger state-of-the-art models.

MetaEarth_A_Generative_Foundation_Model_.title: MetaEarth: A Generative Foundation Model for Global-Scale Remote Sensing Image Generation
MetaEarth_A_Generative_Foundation_Model_.venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
MetaEarth_A_Generative_Foundation_Model_.journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
MetaEarth_A_Generative_Foundation_Model_.tldr: This work proposes a resolution-guided self-cascading generative framework, which enables the generating of images at any region with a wide range of geographical resolutions, and designs a novel noise sampling strategy for denoising diffusion models by analyzing the generation conditions and initial noise.

OReole-FM_successes_and_challenges_towar.title: OReole-FM: successes and challenges toward billion-parameter foundation models for high-resolution satellite imagery
OReole-FM_successes_and_challenges_towar.journal: Proceedings of the 32nd ACM International Conference on Advances in Geographic Information Systems
OReole-FM_successes_and_challenges_towar.tldr: This study assesses performance of different pretrained variants of vision Transformers across image classification, semantic segmentation and object detection benchmarks, which highlight the importance of data scaling for effective model scaling.

OlmoEarth_Stable_Latent_Image_Modeling_f.title: OlmoEarth: Stable Latent Image Modeling for Multimodal Earth Observation
OlmoEarth_Stable_Latent_Image_Modeling_f.tldr: OlmoEarth is a multimodal, spatio-temporal foundation model that employs a novel self-supervised learning formulation, masking strategy, and loss all designed for the Earth observation domain that achieves state-of-the-art performance compared to 12 other foundation models across a variety of research benchmarks and real-world tasks from external partners.

OmniSat_Self-Supervised_Modality_Fusion_.title: OmniSat: Self-Supervised Modality Fusion for Earth Observation
OmniSat_Self-Supervised_Modality_Fusion_.tldr: OmniSat is introduced, a novel architecture able to merge diverse EO modalities into expressive features without labels by exploiting their alignment, leading to state-of-the-art performances in semi- and fully supervised settings.

Panopticon_Advancing_Any-Sensor_Foundati.title: Panopticon: Advancing Any-Sensor Foundation Models for Earth Observation
Panopticon_Advancing_Any-Sensor_Foundati.venue: 2025 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition Workshops (CVPRW)
Panopticon_Advancing_Any-Sensor_Foundati.journal: 2025 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition Workshops (CVPRW)
Panopticon_Advancing_Any-Sensor_Foundati.tldr: Panopticon, an any-sensor foundation model built on the DINOv2 framework, achieves state-of-the-art performance on GEO-Bench, especially on the widely-used Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 sensors, while out-competing other any-sensor models, as well as domain adapted fixed-sensor models on unique sensor configurations.

Presto_Lightweight_Pre-trained_Transform.title: Lightweight, Pre-trained Transformers for Remote Sensing Timeseries
Presto_Lightweight_Pre-trained_Transform.tldr: Presto (the Pretrained Remote Sensing Transformer), a model pre-trained on remote sensing pixel-timeseries data, which excels at a wide variety of globally distributed remote sensing tasks and performs competitively with much larger models while requiring far less compute.

Prithvi-EO-20_A_Versatile_Multi-Temporal.title: Prithvi-EO-2.0: A Versatile Multitemporal Foundation Model for Earth Observation Applications
Prithvi-EO-20_A_Versatile_Multi-Temporal.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
Prithvi-EO-20_A_Versatile_Multi-Temporal.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
Prithvi-EO-20_A_Versatile_Multi-Temporal.tldr: Prithvi-EO-2.0 is a new geospatial foundation model (GFM) that offers significant improvements over its predecessor, Prithvi-EO-1.0, and demonstrates the versatility of the model in both classical Earth observation (EO) and high-resolution applications.

Prithvi_Foundation_Models_for_Generalist.title: Foundation Models for Generalist Geospatial Artificial Intelligence
Prithvi_Foundation_Models_for_Generalist.tldr: A first-of-a-kind framework for the efficient pre-training and fine-tuning of foundational models on extensive geospatial data is introduced and Prithvi, a transformer-based geosp spatial foundational model pre-trained on more than 1TB of multispectral satellite imagery from the Harmonized Landsat-Sentinel 2 (HLS) dataset is created.

Prithvi_WxC_Foundation_Model_for_Weather.title: Prithvi WxC: Foundation Model for Weather and Climate
Prithvi_WxC_Foundation_Model_for_Weather.tldr: Prithvi WxC is introduced, a 2.3 billion parameter foundation model developed using 160 variables from the Modern-Era Retrospective Analysis for Research and Applications, Version 2 (MERRA-2), incorporating concepts from various recent transformer models to effectively capture both regional and global dependencies in the input data.

RS5M_and_GeoRSCLIP_A_Large_Scale_Vision-.title: RS5M and GeoRSCLIP: A Large-Scale Vision- Language Dataset and a Large Vision-Language Model for Remote Sensing
RS5M_and_GeoRSCLIP_A_Large_Scale_Vision-.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
RS5M_and_GeoRSCLIP_A_Large_Scale_Vision-.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
RS5M_and_GeoRSCLIP_A_Large_Scale_Vision-.tldr: An image–text paired dataset in the field of remote sensing (RS), RS5M, which has 5 million RS images with English descriptions is presented, which is the first large-scale RS image–text paired dataset and the model GeoRSCLIP improves upon the baseline or previous state-of-the-art model.

RSGPT_A_Remote_Sensing_Vision_Language_M.title: RSGPT: A Remote Sensing Vision Language Model and Benchmark
RSGPT_A_Remote_Sensing_Vision_Language_M.venue: Isprs Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
RSGPT_A_Remote_Sensing_Vision_Language_M.tldr: A high-quality Remote Sensing Image Captioning dataset (RSICap) is built that facilitates the development of large VLMs in the RS field and provides a benchmark evaluation dataset called RSIEval, which consists of human-annotated captions and visual question-answer pairs, allowing for a comprehensive assessment of VL Ms in the context of RS.

RemoteCLIP_A_Vision_Language_Foundation_.title: RemoteCLIP: A Vision Language Foundation Model for Remote Sensing
RemoteCLIP_A_Vision_Language_Foundation_.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
RemoteCLIP_A_Vision_Language_Foundation_.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
RemoteCLIP_A_Vision_Language_Foundation_.tldr: RemoteCLIP is proposed, the first vision-language foundation model for remote sensing that aims to learn robust visual features with rich semantics and aligned text embeddings for seamless downstream application and consistently outperforms baseline foundation models across different model scales.

Representation_Learning_for_Remote_Sensi.title: Representation Learning for Remote Sensing: An Unsupervised Sensor Fusion Approach
Representation_Learning_for_Remote_Sensi.tldr: This work proposes a new self-supervised training objective, Contrastive Sensor Fusion, which exploits coterminous data from multiple sources to learn useful representations of every possible combination of those sources.

RoMA_Scaling_up_Mamba-based_Foundation_M.title: RoMA: Scaling up Mamba-based Foundation Models for Remote Sensing
RoMA_Scaling_up_Mamba-based_Foundation_M.tldr: RoMA is proposed, a framework that enables scalable self-supervised pretraining of Mamba-based RS foundation models using large-scale, diverse, unlabeled data and demonstrates that RoMA-pretrained Mamba models consistently outperform ViT-based counterparts in both accuracy and computational efficiency.

S2MAE_A_Spatial-Spectral_Pretraining_Fou.title: S2MAE: A Spatial-Spectral Pretraining Foundation Model for Spectral Remote Sensing Data
S2MAE_A_Spatial-Spectral_Pretraining_Fou.journal: 2024 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)

S4_Self-Supervised_Sensing_Across_the_Sp.title: S4: Self-Supervised Sensing Across the Spectrum
S4_Self-Supervised_Sensing_Across_the_Sp.tldr: This work proposes S4 a new self-supervised pre-training approach that significantly reduces the requirement for labeled training data by utilizing two new insights: (a) Satellites capture images in different parts of the spectrum such as radio frequencies, and visible frequencies, and (b) Satellite imagery is geo-registered allowing for fine-grained spatial alignment.

SARATR-X_Toward_Building_a_Foundation_Mo.title: SARATR-X: Toward Building a Foundation Model for SAR Target Recognition
SARATR-X_Toward_Building_a_Foundation_Mo.tldr: SARATR-X learns generalizable representations via self-supervised learning (SSL) and provides a cornerstone for label-efficient model adaptation to generic SAR target detection and classification tasks, and is trained on 0.18 M unlabelled SAR target samples, which are curated by combining contemporary benchmarks and constitute the largest publicly available dataset till now.

SARVLM_A_Vision_Language_Foundation_Mode.title: SARVLM: A Vision Language Foundation Model for Semantic Understanding and Target Recognition in SAR Imagery
SARVLM_A_Vision_Language_Foundation_Mode.tldr: SARVLM is the first vision language foundation model (VLM) tailored to SAR, comprising SARCLIP and SARCap, and is trained with a vision-language contrastive objective under the proposed domain transfer strategy, bridging SAR imagery and textual descriptions.

SS-MAE_Spatial-Spectral_Masked_Autoencod.title: SS-MAE: Spatial–Spectral Masked Autoencoder for Multisource Remote Sensing Image Classification
SS-MAE_Spatial-Spectral_Masked_Autoencod.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
SS-MAE_Spatial-Spectral_Masked_Autoencod.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
SS-MAE_Spatial-Spectral_Masked_Autoencod.tldr: A spatial–spectral masked autoencoder for hyperspectral image (HSI) and light detection and ranging (LiDAR)/synthetic aperture radar (SAR) data joint classification and the superiority of the proposed SS-MAE is verified compared with several state-of-the-art baselines.

SSL4EO-L_Datasets_and_Foundation_Models_.title: SSL4EO-L: Datasets and Foundation Models for Landsat Imagery
SSL4EO-L_Datasets_and_Foundation_Models_.tldr: SSL4EO-L is introduced, the first ever dataset designed for Self-Supervised Learning for Earth Observation for the Landsat family of satellites and the largest Landsat dataset in history (5M image patches).

SSL4EO-S12_A_Large-Scale_Multi-Modal_Mul.title: SSL4EO-S12: A Large-Scale Multi-Modal, Multi-Temporal Dataset for Self-Supervised Learning in Earth Observation
SSL4EO-S12_A_Large-Scale_Multi-Modal_Mul.tldr: For EO applications it is demonstrated SSL4EO-S12 to succeed in self-supervised pre-training for a set of methods: MoCo-v2, DINO, MAE, and data2vec, and resulting models yield downstream performance close to, or surpassing accuracy measures of supervised learning.

SatMAE_Pre-training_Transformers_for_Tem.title: SatMAE: Pre-training Transformers for Temporal and Multi-Spectral Satellite Imagery
SatMAE_Pre-training_Transformers_for_Tem.tldr: SatMAE is presented, a pre-training framework for temporal or multi-spectral satellite imagery based on Masked Autoencoder, which includes a temporal embedding along with independently masking image patches across time and shows strong improvements over previous state-of-the-art techniques.

SatMAE_Rethinking_Transformers_Pre-train.title: Rethinking Transformers Pre-training for Multi-Spectral Satellite Imagery
SatMAE_Rethinking_Transformers_Pre-train.journal: 2024 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
SatMAE_Rethinking_Transformers_Pre-train.tldr: The proposed SatMAE++ with multi-scale pre-training is equally effective for both optical as well as multi-spectral imagery and achieves mean average precision (mAP) gain of 2.5% for multi-label classification task on BigEarthNet dataset.

SatMamba_Development_of_Foundation_Model.title: SatMamba: Development of Foundation Models for Remote Sensing Imagery Using State Space Models
SatMamba_Development_of_Foundation_Model.tldr: SatMamba is proposed, a new pretraining framework that combines masked autoencoders with State Space Model, offering linear computational scaling and paving the way for more efficient foundation models and unlocking the full potential of Earth observation data.

SatSwinMAE_Efficient_Autoencoding_for_Mu.title: SatSwinMAE: Efficient Autoencoding for Multiscale Time-series Satellite Imagery
SatSwinMAE_Efficient_Autoencoding_for_Mu.tldr: The SwinMAE model is extended to integrate temporal information for satellite time-series data, and shows significant performance improvements over existing state-of-the-art foundation models for all the evaluated downstream tasks: land cover segmentation, building density prediction, flood mapping, wildfire scar mapping and multi-temporal crop segmentation.

SatVision-TOA_A_Geospatial_Foundation_Mo.title: SatVision-TOA: A Geospatial Foundation Model for Coarse-Resolution All-Sky Remote Sensing Imagery
SatVision-TOA_A_Geospatial_Foundation_Mo.tldr: SatVision-TOA is introduced, a novel foundation model pre-trained on 14-band MODIS L1B Top-Of-Atmosphere (TOA) radiance imagery, addressing the need for models pre-trained to handle moderate- and coarse-resolution all-sky remote sensing data.

Scale-MAE_A_Scale-Aware_Masked_Autoencod.title: Scale-MAE: A Scale-Aware Masked Autoencoder for Multiscale Geospatial Representation Learning
Scale-MAE_A_Scale-Aware_Masked_Autoencod.venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
Scale-MAE_A_Scale-Aware_Masked_Autoencod.journal: 2023 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
Scale-MAE_A_Scale-Aware_Masked_Autoencod.tldr: Scale-MAE is presented, a pretraining method that explicitly learns relationships between data at different, known scales throughout the pretraining process, and it is found that tasking the network with reconstructing both low/high frequency images leads to robust multiscale representations for remote sensing imagery.

SeaMo_A_Multi-Seasonal_and_Multimodal_Re.title: SeaMo: A season-aware multimodal foundation model for remote sensing
SeaMo_A_Multi-Seasonal_and_Multimodal_Re.tldr: This work introduces SeaMo, a novel VFM that effectively integrates multimodal and multi-seasonal RS information, and leverages a masked image modeling framework to fully exploit the spatial, spectral, and seasonal dimensions of RS data.

Seasonal_Contrast_Unsupervised_Pre-Train.title: Seasonal Contrast: Unsupervised Pre-Training from Uncurated Remote Sensing Data
Seasonal_Contrast_Unsupervised_Pre-Train.venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
Seasonal_Contrast_Unsupervised_Pre-Train.journal: 2021 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
Seasonal_Contrast_Unsupervised_Pre-Train.tldr: This work proposes Seasonal Contrast (SeCo), an effective pipeline to leverage unlabeled data for in-domain pre-training of remote sensing representations and empirically shows that models trained with SeCo achieve better performance than their ImageNet pre-trained counterparts and state-of-the-art self-supervised learning methods on multiple downstream tasks.

Self-Supervised_Learning_of_Remote_Sensi.title: Self-Supervised Learning of Remote Sensing Scene Representations Using Contrastive Multiview Coding
Self-Supervised_Learning_of_Remote_Sensi.venue: 2021 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition Workshops (CVPRW)
Self-Supervised_Learning_of_Remote_Sensi.journal: 2021 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition Workshops (CVPRW)
Self-Supervised_Learning_of_Remote_Sensi.tldr: It is shown that, for the downstream task of remote sensing image classification, using self-supervised pre-training on remote sensing images can give better results than using supervised pre- training on images of natural scenes.

Self-Supervised_Material_and_Texture_Rep.title: Self-Supervised Material and Texture Representation Learning for Remote Sensing Tasks
Self-Supervised_Material_and_Texture_Rep.journal: 2022 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
Self-Supervised_Material_and_Texture_Rep.tldr: This work presents a material and texture based self-supervision method named MATTER (MATerial and TExture Representation Learning), which is inspired by classical material andtexture methods and allows for up to 24.22% and 6.33% performance increase in unsupervised and finetuned setups, and up to 76% faster convergence on change detection, land cover classification, and semantic segmentation tasks.

Semantic_Segmentation_of_Remote_Sensing_.title: Semantic Segmentation of Remote Sensing Images With Self-Supervised Multitask Representation Learning
Semantic_Segmentation_of_Remote_Sensing_.venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
Semantic_Segmentation_of_Remote_Sensing_.journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
Semantic_Segmentation_of_Remote_Sensing_.tldr: This work designs three different pretext tasks and a triplet Siamese network to learn the high-level and low-level image features at the same time and proposes a self-supervised multitask representation learning method to capture effective visual representations of remote sensing images.

SkyEyeGPT_Unifying_Remote_Sensing_Vision.title: SkyEyeGPT: Unifying Remote Sensing Vision-Language Tasks via Instruction Tuning with Large Language Model
SkyEyeGPT_Unifying_Remote_Sensing_Vision.venue: Isprs Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
SkyEyeGPT_Unifying_Remote_Sensing_Vision.tldr: This work introduces SkyEyeGPT, a unified multi-modal large language model specifically designed for RS vision-language understanding, and designs a two-stage tuning method to enhance instruction-following and multi-turn dialogue ability at different granularities.

SkyScript_A_Large_and_Semantically_Diver.title: SkyScript: A Large and Semantically Diverse Vision-Language Dataset for Remote Sensing
SkyScript_A_Large_and_Semantically_Diver.tldr: SkyScript is constructed, a comprehensive vision-language dataset for remote sensing images, comprising 2.6 million image-text pairs covering 29K distinct semantic tags that can support the advancement of VLMs for various multi-modal tasks in remote sensing, such as open-vocabulary classification, retrieval, captioning, and text-to-image synthesis.

SoftCon_Multi-Label_Guided_Soft_Contrast.title: Multilabel-Guided Soft Contrastive Learning for Efficient Earth Observation Pretraining
SoftCon_Multi-Label_Guided_Soft_Contrast.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
SoftCon_Multi-Label_Guided_Soft_Contrast.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
SoftCon_Multi-Label_Guided_Soft_Contrast.tldr: This work proposes soft contrastive learning (SoftCon) that optimizes cross-scene soft similarity based on land-cover-generated multilabel supervision, naturally solving the issue of multiple positive samples and too strict positive matching in complex scenes.

SpectralEarth_Training_Hyperspectral_Fou.title: SpectralEarth: Training Hyperspectral Foundation Models at Scale
SpectralEarth_Training_Hyperspectral_Fou.venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
SpectralEarth_Training_Hyperspectral_Fou.journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
SpectralEarth_Training_Hyperspectral_Fou.tldr: SpectralEarth is introduced, a large-scale multitemporal dataset designed to pretrain hyperspectral foundation models leveraging data from the environmental mapping and analysis program (EnMAP), and a spectral adapter into classical vision backbones to accommodate the unique characteristics of HSI.

TOV_The_Original_Vision_Model_for_Optica.title: TOV: The Original Vision Model for Optical Remote Sensing Image Understanding via Self-Supervised Learning
TOV_The_Original_Vision_Model_for_Optica.venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
TOV_The_Original_Vision_Model_for_Optica.journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
TOV_The_Original_Vision_Model_for_Optica.tldr: It is argued that a more desirable RSIU model should be trained with intrinsic structure from data rather than extrinsic human labels to realize generalizability across a wide range of R SIU tasks.

TerraMind_Large-Scale_Generative_Multimo.title: TerraMind: Large-Scale Generative Multimodality for Earth Observation
TerraMind_Large-Scale_Generative_Multimo.venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
TerraMind_Large-Scale_Generative_Multimo.journal: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
TerraMind_Large-Scale_Generative_Multimo.tldr: It is demonstrated that TerraMind achieves beyond state-of-the-art performance in communitystandard benchmarks, and TerraMind can leverage “thinking in modalities” (TiM) - the capability of generating additional artificial data during finetuning and inference to improve the model output.

The_color_out_of_space_learning_self-sup.title: The color out of space: learning self-supervised representations for Earth Observation imagery
The_color_out_of_space_learning_self-sup.tldr: This work proposes to learn meaningful representations from satellite imagery, leveraging its high-dimensionality spectral bands to reconstruct the visible colors and qualitatively observes that guesses based on natural images and colorization rely on different parts of the input.

Tile2Vec_Unsupervised_Representation_Lea.title: Tile2Vec: Unsupervised representation learning for spatially distributed data
Tile2Vec_Unsupervised_Representation_Lea.tldr: Tile2Vec is introduced, an unsupervised representation learning algorithm that extends the distributional hypothesis from natural language — words appearing in similar contexts tend to have similar meanings — to spatially distributed data to improve performance in downstream classification tasks.

Towards_Scalable_Foundation_Model_for_Mu.title: Towards Scalable Foundation Model for Multi-modal and Hyperspectral Geospatial Data
Towards_Scalable_Foundation_Model_for_Mu.tldr: Low-rank Efficient Spatial-Spectral Vision Transformer is introduced with three key innovations that approximates high-dimensional spatial-spectral attention through Kronecker's product of the low-dimensional spatial and spectral attention components and pretrain LESS ViT using a Hyperspectral Masked Autoencoder framework with integrated positional and channel masking strategies.

Towards_a_Unified_Copernicus_Foundation_.title: Towards a Unified Copernicus Foundation Model for Earth Vision
Towards_a_Unified_Copernicus_Foundation_.venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
Towards_a_Unified_Copernicus_Foundation_.journal: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
Towards_a_Unified_Copernicus_Foundation_.tldr: A unified foundation model capable of processing any spectral or non-spectral sensor modality using extended dynamic hypernetworks and flexible metadata encoding, and a systematic evaluation benchmark with 15 hierarchical downstream tasks ranging from preprocessing to specialized applications for each Sentinel mission.

USat_A_Unified_Self-Supervised_Encoder_f.title: USat: A Unified Self-Supervised Encoder for Multi-Sensor Satellite Imagery
USat_A_Unified_Self-Supervised_Encoder_f.tldr: A new encoder architecture called USat is developed that can input multi-spectral data from multiple sensors for self-supervised pre-training and it is found that a pre-trained USat outperforms state-of-the-art self- supervised MAE models trained on remote sensing data on multiple remote sensing benchmark datasets.

UrbanCLIP_Learning_Text-Enhanced_Urban_R.title: UrbanCLIP: Learning Text-enhanced Urban Region Profiling with Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining from the Web
UrbanCLIP_Learning_Text-Enhanced_Urban_R.journal: Proceedings of the ACM Web Conference 2024
UrbanCLIP_Learning_Text-Enhanced_Urban_R.tldr: This work introduces the first-ever LLM-enhanced framework that integrates the knowledge of text modality into urban imagery, named LLM-enhanced Urban Region Profiling with Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining (UrbanCLIP), and generates a detailed textual description for each satellite image by Image-to-Text LLMs.

WV-Net_A_foundation_model_for_SAR_WV-mod.title: WV-Net: A foundation model for SAR WV-mode satellite imagery trained using contrastive self-supervised learning on 10 million images
WV-Net_A_foundation_model_for_SAR_WV-mod.tldr: It is demonstrated that WV-Net embeddings can support geophysical research by providing a convenient foundation model for a variety of data analysis and exploration tasks and scale better in data-sparse settings.

WildSAT_Learning_Satellite_Image_Represe.title: WildSAT: Learning Satellite Image Representations from Wildlife Observations
WildSAT_Learning_Satellite_Image_Represe.venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
WildSAT_Learning_Satellite_Image_Represe.journal: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
WildSAT_Learning_Satellite_Image_Represe.tldr: This work introduces WildSAT, which pairs satellite images with millions of geo-tagged wildlife observations readily available on citizen science platforms, and employs a contrastive learning approach that significantly improves performance on diverse satellite image recognition tasks, outperforming both ImageNet-pretrained models and satellite-specific baselines.

A_generalizable_and_accessible_approach_.title: A generalizable and accessible approach to machine learning with global satellite imagery
A_generalizable_and_accessible_approach_.tldr: MOSAIKS generalizes across prediction domains and has the potential to enhance accessibility of SIML across research disciplines and achieves accuracy competitive with deep neural networks at orders of magnitude lower computational cost.

Contrastive_Self-Supervised_Data_Fusion_.title: CONTRASTIVE SELF-SUPERVISED DATA FUSION FOR SATELLITE IMAGERY
Contrastive_Self-Supervised_Data_Fusion_.venue: ISPRS Annals of the Photogrammetry, Remote Sensing and Spatial Information Sciences
Contrastive_Self-Supervised_Data_Fusion_.journal: ISPRS Annals of the Photogrammetry, Remote Sensing and Spatial Information Sciences
Contrastive_Self-Supervised_Data_Fusion_.tldr: The results show that contrastive data fusion is a powerful self-supervised technique to train image encoders that are capable of producing meaningful representations: Simple linear probing performs on par with fully supervised approaches and fine-tuning with as little as 10% of the labelled data results in higher accuracy than supervised training on the entire dataset.

Geographical_Supervision_Correction_for_.title: Geographical Supervision Correction for Remote Sensing Representation Learning
Geographical_Supervision_Correction_for_.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
Geographical_Supervision_Correction_for_.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

RS-BYOL_Self-Supervised_Learning_for_Inv.title: Self-Supervised Learning for Invariant Representations From Multi-Spectral and SAR Images
RS-BYOL_Self-Supervised_Learning_for_Inv.venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
RS-BYOL_Self-Supervised_Learning_for_Inv.journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
RS-BYOL_Self-Supervised_Learning_for_Inv.tldr: This work proposes RS-BYOL, which builds on BYOL in the remote sensing (RS) domain where data are nontrivially different from natural RGB images, and explores the usefulness of single channel feature learning from random 10 MS bands of 10–20 m resolution and VV-VH of SAR bands compared to the common notion of using three or more bands.

RingMo-Aerial_An_Aerial_Remote_Sensing_F.title: RingMo-Aerial: An Aerial Remote Sensing Foundation Model With Affine Transformation Contrastive Learning
RingMo-Aerial_An_Aerial_Remote_Sensing_F.venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
RingMo-Aerial_An_Aerial_Remote_Sensing_F.journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
RingMo-Aerial_An_Aerial_Remote_Sensing_F.tldr: Experimental results demonstrate that RingMo-Aerial achieves SOTA performance on multiple downstream tasks, indicating the practicality and efficacy of RingMo-Aerial in enhancing the performance of ARS vision tasks.

RingMo-Lite_A_Remote_Sensing_Lightweight.title: RingMo-lite: A Remote Sensing Multi-task Lightweight Network with CNN-Transformer Hybrid Framework
RingMo-Lite_A_Remote_Sensing_Lightweight.tldr: This paper proposes RingMo-lite, an RS multi-task lightweight network with a CNN-Transformer hybrid framework, which effectively exploits the frequency-domain properties of RS to optimize the interpretation process.

Self-Supervised_Vision_Transformers_for_.title: Self-Supervised Vision Transformers for Joint SAR-Optical Representation Learning
Self-Supervised_Vision_Transformers_for_.venue: IEEE International Geoscience and Remote Sensing Symposium
Self-Supervised_Vision_Transformers_for_.journal: IGARSS 2022 - 2022 IEEE International Geoscience and Remote Sensing Symposium
Self-Supervised_Vision_Transformers_for_.tldr: This work combines SAR and optical imagery by concatenating all channels to a unified input and randomly masks out channels of one modality as a data augmentation strategy in a self-supervised SSL algorithm based on DINO.

Self-supervised_Audiovisual_Representati.title: Self-supervised Audiovisual Representation Learning for Remote Sensing Data
Self-supervised_Audiovisual_Representati.venue: International Journal of Applied Earth Observation and Geoinformation
Self-supervised_Audiovisual_Representati.journal: Int. J. Appl. Earth Obs. Geoinformation
Self-supervised_Audiovisual_Representati.tldr: The SoundingEarth dataset is introduced, which consists of co-located aerial imagery and audio samples all around the world, and a self-supervised approach for pre-training deep neural networks is devised, which outperforms existing pre-training strategies for remote sensing imagery.

SpectralGPT_Spectral_Remote_Sensing_Foun.title: SpectralGPT: Spectral Remote Sensing Foundation Model
SpectralGPT_Spectral_Remote_Sensing_Foun.venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
SpectralGPT_Spectral_Remote_Sensing_Foun.journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
SpectralGPT_Spectral_Remote_Sensing_Foun.tldr: This work has created for the first time a universal RS foundation model, named SpectralGPT, which is purpose-built to handle spectral RS images using a novel 3D generative pretrained transformer (GPT).

GeoKR.pdf.title: Geographical Knowledge-Driven Representation Learning for Remote Sensing Images
GeoKR.pdf.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
GeoKR.pdf.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
GeoKR.pdf.tldr: Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed Geographical Knowledge-driven Representation (GeoKR) learning method outperforms ImageNet pretraining and self-supervised representation learning methods and significantly reduces the burden of data annotation on downstream tasks, such as scene classification, semantic segmentation, object detection, and cloud/snow detection.

RSP.pdf.title: An Empirical Study of Remote Sensing Pretraining
RSP.pdf.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
RSP.pdf.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
RSP.pdf.tldr: Empirical study shows that RSP can help deliver distinctive performances in scene recognition tasks and in perceiving RS related semantics such as “Bridge” and “Airplane”, and finds that, although RSP mitigates the data discrepancies of traditional ImageNet pretraining on RS images, it may still suffer from task discrepancies, where downstream tasks require different representations from scene Recognition tasks.

RVSA.pdf.title: Advancing Plain Vision Transformer Toward Remote Sensing Foundation Model
RVSA.pdf.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
RVSA.pdf.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
RVSA.pdf.tldr: This article proposes a new rotated varied-size window attention to replace the original full attention in transformers, which can significantly reduce the computational cost and memory footprint while learning better object representation by extracting rich context from the generated diverse windows.

CSPT.pdf.title: Consecutive Pretraining: A Knowledge Transfer Learning Strategy with Relevant Unlabeled Data for Remote Sensing Domain
CSPT.pdf.tldr: By utilizing the proposed CSPT for task-aware model training, almost all downstream tasks in the RSD can outperform the previous knowledge transfer learning strategies based on model pre-training without any expensive manually labeling and even surpass the state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance without any careful network architecture designing.

GFM.pdf.title: Towards Geospatial Foundation Models via Continual Pretraining
GFM.pdf.venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
GFM.pdf.journal: 2023 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
GFM.pdf.tldr: This work investigates the potential of continual pretraining from large-scale ImageNet-22k models and proposes a multi-objective continual pretraining paradigm, which leverages the strong representations of ImageNet while simultaneously providing the freedom to learn valuable in-domain features.

SwiMDiff.pdf.title: SwiMDiff: Scene-Wide Matching Contrastive Learning With Diffusion Constraint for Remote Sensing Image
SwiMDiff.pdf.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
SwiMDiff.pdf.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
SwiMDiff.pdf.tldr: This work introduces SwiMDiff, a novel self-supervised pretraining framework designed for RSIs that employs a scene-wide matching approach that effectively recalibrates labels to recognize data from the same scene as false negatives, and enhances the encoder’s ability to capture both the global semantic information and fine-grained features of the images more comprehensively.

SAR-JEPA.pdf.title: Predicting gradient is better: Exploring self-supervised learning for SAR ATR with a joint-embedding predictive architecture
SAR-JEPA.pdf.venue: Isprs Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
SAR-JEPA.pdf.journal: ISPRS Journal of Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing
SAR-JEPA.pdf.tldr: This study investigates an effective SSL method for SAR ATR, which can pave the way for a foundation model in SAR ATR and presents a novel Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture for SAR ATR (SAR-JEPA), which leverages local masked patches to predict the multi-scale SAR gradient representations of unseen context.

SkySense.pdf.title: SkySense: A Multi-Modal Remote Sensing Foundation Model Towards Universal Interpretation for Earth Observation Imagery
SkySense.pdf.journal: 2024 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
SkySense.pdf.tldr: SkySense is the largest Multi-Modal RSFM to date, whose modules can be flexibly combined or used individually to accommodate various tasks, and demonstrates remarkable generalization capabilities on a thor-ough evaluation encompassing 16 datasets over 7 tasks.

SemanticAware.pdf.title: Semantic-Aware Dense Representation Learning for Remote Sensing Image Change Detection
SemanticAware.pdf.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
SemanticAware.pdf.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
SemanticAware.pdf.tldr: Different from traditional supervised pretraining that learns the mapping from image to label, semantic supervision is incorporated into the self-supervised learning (SSL) framework and significantly outperforms ImageNet pretraining, in-domain supervision, and several SSL methods.

HyperSIGMA.pdf.title: HyperSIGMA: Hyperspectral Intelligence Comprehension Foundation Model
HyperSIGMA.pdf.venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
HyperSIGMA.pdf.journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
HyperSIGMA.pdf.tldr: A novel sparse sampling attention (SSA) mechanism is introduced, which effectively promotes the learning of diverse contextual features and serves as the basic block of HyperSIGMA, a vision transformer-based foundation model that unifies HSI interpretation across tasks and scenes, scalable to over one billion parameters.

RingMoE.pdf.title: RingMoE: Mixture-of-Modality-Experts Multi-Modal Foundation Models for Universal Remote Sensing Image Interpretation
RingMoE.pdf.venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
RingMoE.pdf.journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

RingMo-Agent.pdf.title: RingMo-Agent: A Unified Remote Sensing Foundation Model for Multi-Platform and Multi-Modal Reasoning
RingMo-Agent.pdf.tldr: RingMo-Agent is proposed, a model designed to handle multi-modal and multi-platform data that performs perception and reasoning tasks based on user textual instructions that proves effective in both visual understanding and sophisticated analytical tasks, and exhibits strong generalizability across different platforms and sensing modalities.

RS-DFM.pdf.title: RS-DFM: A Remote Sensing Distributed Foundation Model for Diverse Downstream Tasks
RS-DFM.pdf.tldr: A Remote Sensing Distributed Foundation Model based on generalized information mapping and interaction that can realize online collaborative perception across multiple platforms and various downstream tasks by mapping observations into a unified space and implementing a task-agnostic information interaction strategy.

HSIGene.pdf.title: HSIGene: A Foundation Model for Hyperspectral Image Generation
HSIGene.pdf.venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
HSIGene.pdf.journal: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
HSIGene.pdf.tldr: HSIGene is proposed, a novel HSI generation foundation model which is based on latent diffusion and supports multi-condition control, allowing for more precise and reliable HSI generation and to enhance the spatial diversity of the training data while preserving spectral fidelity.

RS-vHeat.pdf.title: RS-vHeat: Heat Conduction Guided Efficient Remote Sensing Foundation Model
RS-vHeat.pdf.venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
RS-vHeat.pdf.journal: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
RS-vHeat.pdf.tldr: This work is the first to explore the potential of using the parallel computing model of heat conduction to simulate the local region correlations in high-resolution remote sensing images, and introduces RS-vHeat, an efficient multi-modal remote sensing foundation model.

SatDiFuser.pdf.title: Can Generative Geospatial Diffusion Models Excel as Discriminative Geospatial Foundation Models?
SatDiFuser.pdf.venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
SatDiFuser.pdf.journal: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
SatDiFuser.pdf.tldr: SatDiFuser is a framework that transforms a diffusion-based generative geospatial foundation model into a powerful pretraining tool for discriminative RS, and develops three fusion strategies to effectively leverage these diverse representations.

CMID.pdf.title: CMID: A Unified Self-Supervised Learning Framework for Remote Sensing Image Understanding
CMID.pdf.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
CMID.pdf.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
CMID.pdf.tldr: The proposed SSL framework, contrastive mask image distillation (CMID), is capable of learning representations with both global semantic separability and local spatial perceptibility by combining contrastive learning with masked image modeling (MIM) in a self-distillation way.

GRAFT.pdf.title: Remote Sensing Vision-Language Foundation Models without Annotations via Ground Remote Alignment
GRAFT.pdf.tldr: This work introduces a method to train vision-language models for remote-sensing images without using any textual annotations, and shows that these VLMs enable zero-shot, open-vocabulary image classification, retrieval, segmentation and visual question answering for satellite images.

DynamicVis.pdf.title: DynamicVis: Dynamic Visual Perception for Efficient Remote Sensing Foundation Models
DynamicVis.pdf.tldr: This work proposes DynamicVis, a visual foundation model explicitly tailored to the sparse nature of RS imagery, and proposes a novel Region-Level Meta-Embedding Multi-Instance Learning (MIL) pre-training paradigm, trained on a million-scale dataset.

AlphaEarth.pdf.title: AlphaEarth Foundations: An embedding field model for accurate and efficient global mapping from sparse label data
AlphaEarth.pdf.tldr: AlphaEarth Foundations is introduced, an embedding field model yielding a highly general, geospatial representation that assimilates spatial, temporal, and measurement contexts across multiple sources, enabling accurate and efficient production of maps and monitoring systems from local to global scales.

TiMo.pdf.title: TiMo: Spatiotemporal Foundation Model for Satellite Image Time Series
TiMo.pdf.tldr: Extensive experiments across multiple spatiotemporal tasks-including deforestation monitoring, land cover segmentation, crop type classification, crop type classification, and flood detection-demonstrate TiMo's superiority over state-of-the-art methods.

CGEarthEye.pdf.title: CGEarthEye:A High-Resolution Remote Sensing Vision Foundation Model Based on the Jilin-1 Satellite Constellation
CGEarthEye.pdf.tldr: This study proposes CGEarthEye, a RSVFM framework specifically designed for Jilin-1 satellite characteristics, comprising five backbones with different parameter scales with totaling 2.1 billion parameters, and develops JLSSD, the first 15-million-scale multi-temporal self-supervised learning (SSL) dataset featuring global coverage with quarterly temporal sampling within a single year.

MAPEX.pdf.title: MAPEX: Modality-Aware Pruning of Experts for Remote Sensing Foundation Models
MAPEX.pdf.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
MAPEX.pdf.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

SkySense-V2.pdf.title: SkySense V2: A Unified Foundation Model for Multi-Modal Remote Sensing
SkySense-V2.pdf.venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
SkySense-V2.pdf.journal: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
SkySense-V2.pdf.tldr: SkySense V2 is presented, a unified MM-RSFM that employs a single transformer backbone to handle multiple modalities and is pre-trained with a novel SSL strategy tailored to the distinct traits of RS data.

TESSERA.pdf.title: TESSERA: Temporal Embeddings of Surface Spectra for Earth Representation and Analysis
TESSERA.pdf.tldr: TESSERA is presented, a pixel-wise foundation model for multi-modal (Sentinel-1/2) EO time series that learns robust, label-efficient embeddings that deliver state-of-the-art accuracy with high label efficiency for diverse classification, segmentation, and regression tasks.

CRS-Diff.pdf.title: CRS-Diff: Controllable Remote Sensing Image Generation With Diffusion Model
CRS-Diff.pdf.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
CRS-Diff.pdf.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
CRS-Diff.pdf.tldr: CRS-Diff is the first multiple-condition controllable RS generative model that can simultaneously support text-condition, metadata-condition, and image-condition control inputs, thus enabling more precise control to refine the generation process.

SenPa-MAE.pdf.title: SenPa-MAE: Sensor Parameter Aware Masked Autoencoder for Multi-Satellite Self-Supervised Pretraining
SenPa-MAE.pdf.tldr: This paper introduces SenPa-MAE, a transformer architecture that encodes the sensor parameters of an observed multispectral signal into the image embeddings and paves the way towards a sensor-independent Earth observation foundation model.

OFA-Net.pdf.title: One for All: Toward Unified Foundation Models for Earth Vision
OFA-Net.pdf.venue: IEEE International Geoscience and Remote Sensing Symposium
OFA-Net.pdf.journal: IGARSS 2024 - 2024 IEEE International Geoscience and Remote Sensing Symposium
OFA-Net.pdf.tldr: A simple yet effective method, termed OFA-Net (One-For-All Network): employing a single, shared Transformer backbone for multiple data modalities with different spatial resolutions, forging a path towards a unified foundation backbone model in Earth vision.

LeMeViT.pdf.title: LeMeViT: Efficient Vision Transformer with Learnable Meta Tokens for Remote Sensing Image Interpretation
LeMeViT.pdf.venue: International Joint Conference on Artificial Intelligence
LeMeViT.pdf.tldr: Dual Cross-Attention (DCA) is proposed to promote information exchange between image tokens and meta tokens, where they serve as query and key (value) tokens alternatively in a dual-branch structure, significantly reducing the computational complexity compared to self-attention.

SMARTIES.pdf.title: SMARTIES: Spectrum-Aware Multi-Sensor Auto-Encoder for Remote Sensing Images
SMARTIES.pdf.venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
SMARTIES.pdf.journal: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
SMARTIES.pdf.tldr: SMARTIES is introduced, a generic and versatile foundation model lifting sensor-specific/dependent efforts and enabling scalability and generalization to diverse RS sensors: SMARTIES projects data from heterogeneous sensors into a shared spectrum-aware space, enabling the use of arbitrary combinations of bands both for training and inference.

TerraFM.pdf.title: TerraFM: A Scalable Foundation Model for Unified Multisensor Earth Observation
TerraFM.pdf.tldr: TerraFM is introduced, a scalable self-supervised learning model that leverages globally distributed Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 imagery, combined with large spatial tiles and land-cover aware sampling to enrich spatial and semantic coverage.

SelectiveMAE.pdf.title: Harnessing Massive Satellite Imagery with Efficient Masked Image Modeling
SelectiveMAE.pdf.venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
SelectiveMAE.pdf.journal: 2025 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
SelectiveMAE.pdf.tldr: A new pre-training pipeline for RS models is presented, featuring the creation of a large-scale RS dataset and an efficient MIM approach, and SelectiveMAE, a pre-training method that dynamically encodes and reconstructs semantically rich patch tokens, thereby reducing the inefficiencies of traditional MIM models caused by redundant background pixels in RS images.

PIEViT.pdf.title: Pattern Integration and Enhancement Vision Transformer for Self-Supervised Learning in Remote Sensing
PIEViT.pdf.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
PIEViT.pdf.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
PIEViT.pdf.tldr: This work presents the pattern integration and enhancement vision transformer (PIEViT), a novel SSL framework designed specifically for RS imagery that achieves excellent results in object detection, land cover classification, and change detection, underscoring its robustness, generalization, and transferability for RS image interpretation tasks.

U-BARN.pdf.title: Self-Supervised Spatio-Temporal Representation Learning of Satellite Image Time Series
U-BARN.pdf.venue: IEEE International Geoscience and Remote Sensing Symposium
U-BARN.pdf.journal: IGARSS 2023 - 2023 IEEE International Geoscience and Remote Sensing Symposium

MAESTRO.pdf.title: MAESTRO: Masked AutoEncoders for Multimodal, Multitemporal, and Multispectral Earth Observation Data
MAESTRO.pdf.venue: IEEE Workshop/Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision
MAESTRO.pdf.journal: 2026 IEEE/CVF Winter Conference on Applications of Computer Vision (WACV)
MAESTRO.pdf.tldr: MAESTRO is introduced, a novel adaptation of the Masked Autoencoder with optimized fusion mechanisms and a normalization scheme that incorporates a spectral prior as a self-supervisory signal for multimodal, multitemporal, and multispectral Earth observation data.

GASSL.pdf.title: Geography-Aware Self-Supervised Learning
GASSL.pdf.venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
GASSL.pdf.journal: 2021 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
GASSL.pdf.tldr: This paper proposes novel training methods that exploit the spatio-temporal structure of remote sensing data and leverage spatially aligned images over time to construct temporal positive pairs in contrastive learning and geo-location to design pre-text tasks.

TEOChat.pdf.title: TEOChat: A Large Vision-Language Assistant for Temporal Earth Observation Data
TEOChat.pdf.tldr: This work develops a new vision and language assistant called TEOChat that can engage in conversations about temporal sequences of earth observation data and exhibits stronger single image capabilities than a comparable single image instruction-following model on scene classification, visual question answering, and captioning.

EarthMarker.pdf.title: EarthMarker: A Visual Prompting Multimodal Large Language Model for Remote Sensing
EarthMarker.pdf.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
EarthMarker.pdf.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
EarthMarker.pdf.tldr: The first visual prompting-based multimodal large language model (MLLM) named EarthMarker is proposed in the RS domain, and represents a significant advance in multigranularity RS imagery interpretation under the visual prompting learning framework.

SkySenseGPT.pdf.title: SkySenseGPT: A Fine-Grained Instruction Tuning Dataset and Model for Remote Sensing Vision-Language Understanding
SkySenseGPT.pdf.tldr: A large-scale instruction tuning dataset FIT-RS is proposed, containing 1,800,851 instruction samples, to unlock RSLMMs' complex comprehension ability and establish a new benchmark to evaluate the fine-grained relation comprehension capabilities of LMMs, named FIT-RSRC.

SatlasPretrain.pdf.title: SatlasPretrain: A Large-Scale Dataset for Remote Sensing Image Understanding
SatlasPretrain.pdf.venue: IEEE International Conference on Computer Vision
SatlasPretrain.pdf.journal: 2023 IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
SatlasPretrain.pdf.tldr: This paper presents SatlasPretrain, a remote sensing dataset that is large in both breadth and scale, combining Sentinel-2 and NAIP images with 302M labels under 137 categories and seven label types, and finds that pre-training on SatlasPretrain substantially improves performance on downstream tasks, increasing average accuracy by 18% over ImageNet and 6% over the next best baseline.

LHRS-Bot-Nova.pdf.title: LHRS-Bot-Nova: Improved Multimodal Large Language Model for Remote Sensing Vision-Language Interpretation
LHRS-Bot-Nova.pdf.tldr: LHRS-Bot-Nova is introduced, an MLLM specialized in understanding remote sensing images, designed to expertly perform a wide range of RS understanding tasks aligned with human instructions, and an instruction dataset specifically designed to improve spatial recognition abilities is introduced.

BillionPFM.pdf.title: A Billion-scale Foundation Model for Remote Sensing Images
BillionPFM.pdf.venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
BillionPFM.pdf.tldr: This paper examines the effect of increasing the number of model parameters on the performance of foundation models in downstream tasks such as rotated object detection and semantic segmentation and proposes an effective method for scaling up and fine-tuning a vision transformer in the remote sensing field.

CSMAE.pdf.title: Exploring Masked Autoencoders for Sensor-Agnostic Image Retrieval in Remote Sensing
CSMAE.pdf.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
CSMAE.pdf.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
CSMAE.pdf.tldr: This article takes the first step to explore the effectiveness of MAEs for sensor-agnostic CBIR in RS, and presents a systematic overview on the possible adaptations of the vanilla MAE to exploit masked image modeling (MIM) on multisensor RS image archives in the context of CBIR.

GeoPixel.pdf.title: GeoPixel: Pixel Grounding Large Multimodal Model in Remote Sensing
GeoPixel.pdf.tldr: GeoPixel is proposed - the first end-to-end high resolution RS-LMM that supports pixel-level grounding, and demonstrates superior performance in pixel-level comprehension, surpassing existing LMMs in both single-target and multi-target segmentation tasks.

MMEarth.pdf.title: MMEarth: Exploring Multi-Modal Pretext Tasks For Geospatial Representation Learning
MMEarth.pdf.tldr: This work proposes a Multi-Pretext Masked Autoencoder (MP-MAE) approach to learn general-purpose representations for optical satellite images and demonstrates that this approach outperforms both MAEs pretrained on ImageNet and MAEs pretrained on domain-specific satellite images.

S2-CGAN_SSL_Adversarial_Repr_for_Binary_.title: S2-CGAN: Self-Supervised Adversarial Representation Learning for Binary Change Detection in Multispectral Images
S2-CGAN_SSL_Adversarial_Repr_for_Binary_.venue: IEEE International Geoscience and Remote Sensing Symposium
S2-CGAN_SSL_Adversarial_Repr_for_Binary_.journal: IGARSS 2020 - 2020 IEEE International Geoscience and Remote Sensing Symposium
S2-CGAN_SSL_Adversarial_Repr_for_Binary_.tldr: A self-supervised conditional Generative Adversarial Network trained to generate only the distribution of unchanged samples that directly exploits the discriminator likelihood to solve the binary CD task.

SSL_pretraining_enhances_CD_in_Sentinel2.title: Self-supervised pre-training enhances change detection in Sentinel-2 imagery
SSL_pretraining_enhances_CD_in_Sentinel2.tldr: This work builds and makes publicly available the Sentinel-2 Multitemporal Cities Pairs (S2MTCP) dataset, containing multitemporal image pairs from 1520 urban areas worldwide, and test the results of multiple self-supervised learning methods for pre-training models for change detection on a public change detection dataset made of Sentinel- 2 image pairs (OSCD).

SSL_Multisensor_Change_Detection.pdf.title: Self-Supervised Multisensor Change Detection
SSL_Multisensor_Change_Detection.pdf.venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
SSL_Multisensor_Change_Detection.pdf.journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
SSL_Multisensor_Change_Detection.pdf.tldr: This work proposes a method for multisensor CD using only the unlabeled target bitemporal images that are used for training a network in a self-supervised fashion by using deep clustering and contrastive learning.

SSL_CD_in_Multiview_RS_Images.pdf.title: Multi-task contrastive learning for change detection in remote sensing images
SSL_CD_in_Multiview_RS_Images.pdf.tldr: A novel multi-task contrastive learning (MTCL) approach for change detection of high-resolution remote sensing images that directly utilizes image pairs to train the change detection backbone, further improving the performance of change detection network.

TaskRelated_SSL_for_RS_Image_CD.pdf.title: Task-Related Self-Supervised Learning For Remote Sensing Image Change Detection
TaskRelated_SSL_for_RS_Image_CD.pdf.venue: IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech, and Signal Processing
TaskRelated_SSL_for_RS_Image_CD.pdf.journal: ICASSP 2021 - 2021 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP)
TaskRelated_SSL_for_RS_Image_CD.pdf.tldr: An unsupervised change detection method based on Task-related Self-supervised Learning Change Detection network with smooth mechanism TSLCD is proposed to eliminate the problem of inadequate pseudo-changes suppression and insufficient feature representation.

SSL_RS_Images_CD_at_Pixel_level.pdf.title: Self-supervised Remote Sensing Images Change Detection at Pixel-level
SSL_RS_Images_CD_at_Pixel_level.pdf.tldr: A pixel-wise contrastive approach is proposed to overcome the effects of regular seasonal changes in binary change maps by using contrastive loss in pixel-level features on an unlabeled multi-view setting.

CMC_with_EO_for_SAR_Semantic_Segmentatio.title: Contrastive Multiview Coding With Electro-Optics for SAR Semantic Segmentation
CMC_with_EO_for_SAR_Semantic_Segmentatio.venue: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
CMC_with_EO_for_SAR_Semantic_Segmentatio.journal: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
CMC_with_EO_for_SAR_Semantic_Segmentatio.tldr: This work proposes multimodal representation learning for SAR semantic segmentation from contrastive multiview coding, and several experiments show that this approach is superior to the existing methods in model performance, sample efficiency, and convergence speed.

Continual_Barlow_Twins_for_RS_Semantic_S.title: Continual Barlow Twins: Continual Self-Supervised Learning for Remote Sensing Semantic Segmentation
Continual_Barlow_Twins_for_RS_Semantic_S.venue: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
Continual_Barlow_Twins_for_RS_Semantic_S.journal: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
Continual_Barlow_Twins_for_RS_Semantic_S.tldr: A new algorithm for merging SSL and CL for remote sensing applications that is presented, which combines the advantages of one of the simplest self-supervision techniques, i.e., Barlow twins, with the elastic weight consolidation method to avoid catastrophic forgetting.

SSL_for_Few_Shot_Hyperspectral_Classific.title: Self Supervised Learning for Few Shot Hyperspectral Image Classification
SSL_for_Few_Shot_Hyperspectral_Classific.venue: IEEE International Geoscience and Remote Sensing Symposium
SSL_for_Few_Shot_Hyperspectral_Classific.journal: IGARSS 2022 - 2022 IEEE International Geoscience and Remote Sensing Symposium
SSL_for_Few_Shot_Hyperspectral_Classific.tldr: Self Supervised Learning is proposed to leverage for HSI classification by pre-training an encoder on unlabeled pixels using Barlow-Twins, a state-of-the-art SSL algorithm, and results demonstrate that this approach significantly outperforms vanilla supervised learning.

ContrastCLIP_RS_groundlevel_satellite_pr.title: Contrastive ground-level image and remote sensing pre-training improves representation learning for natural world imagery
ContrastCLIP_RS_groundlevel_satellite_pr.tldr: It is shown how leveraging multiple views of image data with contrastive learning can improve downstream fine-grained classification performance for species recognition, even when one view is absent.
